2013-07-01.title: A Short Lexicon of Italian Gestures
2013-07-01.abstract: For Italians, it comes naturally. But what do they mean when they talk with their hands? Many things. Roll over the images to learn a few classic gestures.

2013-07-01.title: Italian Hand Gestures: A Short History
2013-07-01.abstract: Can Italians talk without using their hands? Gestures that insult, beg and swear offer a window into Roman culture.

2013-07-01.title: Brazilian Police Break Up a Protest
2013-07-01.abstract: Security forces used rubber bullets and tear gas to disperse demonstrators outside a soccer match in Rio de Janeiro.

2013-07-01.title: When Italians Chat, Hands and Fingers Do the Talking
2013-07-01.abstract: Gesticulating is a vital part of communication in Italy, where about 250 gestures are used regularly and studied by scientists and philosophers.

2013-07-01.title: Violent Episodes Grow in Tanzania, an African Haven
2013-07-01.abstract: Human rights groups and the largest opposition party say episodes of intimidation and suppression are growing in the country, an island of stability in an often-chaotic region.

2013-07-02.title: Ousted European Health Official Faces Questions About Plans to Transfer Money
2013-07-02.abstract: The European Anti-Fraud Office is investigating trips by a former European Union official, during which he said he had planned to transfer large sums of money.

2013-07-02.title: When a City Is on the Move, With Mattresses and Dishwashers in Tow
2013-07-02.abstract: Monday was moving day in Montreal for 115,000 people who were sticking with a tradition dating to French rule, when all leases were required to begin on the same day.

2013-07-02.title: Young Activists Rouse Egypt Protests but Leave Next Steps to Public
2013-07-02.abstract: A movement in Egypt illustrates the greatest strengths and the most glaring weaknesses of the youth groups that have driven many of Egypt’s most fundamental political transformations.

2013-07-02.title: Chaos in Middle East Grows as the U.S. Focuses on Israel
2013-07-02.abstract: As protests roil the Middle East, Secretary of State John Kerry’s focus on talks between the Israelis and Palestinians has raised questions about the administration’s priorities.

2013-07-02.title: For Many in Africa, Lack of Electricity Is Barrier to Growth
2013-07-02.abstract: President Obama has begun a new push to bring power to the two-thirds of sub-Saharan Africans who do not have access to it.

2013-07-03.title: After Obama’s Visit, an Electric Moment for Tanzania Lingers
2013-07-03.abstract: President Obama retains the kind of celebrity status in East Africa that he once enjoyed in Europe and other parts of the world, making his visit a public event as much as an act of diplomacy.

2013-07-03.title: Timeline of Turmoil in Egypt From Mubarak and Morsi to Sisi
2013-07-03.abstract: Egypt’s three-year experiment with democracy has ousted Hosni Mubarak and deposed Mohamed Morsi, the country’s first elected leader. Explore key moments of their rule and the aftermath.

2013-07-03.title: Two of Pluto’s Moons Get Names From Greek Mythology’s Underworld
2013-07-03.abstract: After an online contest, an astronomical union selected the names: Styx, a river souls had to cross to get to Hades, and Kerberos, the many-headed guard dog.

2013-07-03.title: Muslims on Guard in Myanmar
2013-07-03.abstract: In Yangon, Myanmar, the Muslim community is on edge. Many worry the recent violence between Buddhists and Muslims elsewhere in the country will arrive in their city.

2013-07-03.title: In Myanmar, Suspicion and Fear Among Neighbors
2013-07-03.abstract: After decades of peaceful coexistence with the Buddhist majority in the country, Muslims say they now live with fear and anxiety about the next attack by rampaging mobs.

2013-07-04.title: Iran’s Next Leader Advocates a Less Intrusive State
2013-07-04.abstract: Iran’s president-elect, Hassan Rowhani, said the government should not meddle in people’s lives, effectively rebuking President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad and the hard-liners who used to support him.

2013-07-04.title: State Department Urges U.S. Citizens to Leave Egypt
2013-07-04.abstract: The United States raised its travel warning because of the political upheavals in Egypt and the possibility of lethal violence.

2013-07-04.title: Rift Over Austerity Plans Is Seen in Ailing Portugal
2013-07-04.abstract: Disagreements over how to mend an ailing economy weakened yet another European coalition government.

2013-07-04.title: For Brazilian Official and Family, Controversy Over a Trip
2013-07-04.abstract: In a nation riven by protests over corruption and overspending, a lawmaker used an Air Force jet to bring guests to the Spain-Brazil soccer match.

2013-07-04.title: Development Project in Istanbul, Focus of Violent Protests, Is Stopped by Court
2013-07-04.abstract: An Istanbul court on Wednesday halted a government-backed plan to rebuild Taksim Square, a development project that incited nearly three weeks of huge antigovernment protests in June.

2013-07-05.title: Résumé Shows Snowden Honed Hacking Skills
2013-07-05.abstract: Edward J. Snowden’s résumé suggests that he transformed himself into the kind of cybersecurity expert the National Security Agency is desperate to recruit.

2013-07-05.title: Brazil Leader Suffers Setback on Overhaul
2013-07-05.abstract: Leaders of President Dilma Rousseff’s governing coalition agreed to pursue a plebiscite on political reforms but fell far shy of meeting her call to hold one right away.

2013-07-05.title: Seeking Sexual Surrogates
2013-07-05.abstract: In France, sexual surrogates for disabled people are not permitted, but some are pushing to make the practice legal.

2013-07-05.title: Disabled People Say They, Too, Want a Sex Life, and Seek Help in Attaining It
2013-07-05.abstract: Some legislators and advocacy groups in France are demanding the legalization of surrogates to help disabled people have sex, but critics see a kind of prostitution.

2013-07-05.title: Greece: Parliamentary Group Advises Criminal Charges for Ex-Finance Chief
2013-07-05.abstract: A Greek parliamentary committee on Thursday recommended that former Finance Minister George Papaconstantinou be indicted on criminal charges.

2013-07-06.title: In Okinawa, Talk of Break From Japan Turns Serious
2013-07-06.abstract: Discontent with American presence and a growing perception that the central government is ignoring pleas to reduce it have made Okinawans willing to flirt with the idea of breaking away.

2013-07-06.title: Missile Defense Interceptor Misses Target in Test
2013-07-06.abstract: Some skeptics of the missile-defense program saw the failure as further evidence that the technology was unreliable and might not be worth the expense.

2013-07-06.title: Chile: Rape Ignites Abortion Debate
2013-07-06.abstract: The case of a pregnant 11-year old girl who was raped has set off a national debate about abortion in one of the most socially conservative countries in Latin America.

2013-07-06.title: Mapping Protests in Cairo
2013-07-06.abstract: Thousands have taken to the streets of Cairo to protest or support deposed President Mohamed Morsi.

2013-07-06.title: Where Mandela Kept Hope, Guide Tells Their Shared Saga
2013-07-06.abstract: Ahmed Kathrada, a longtime anti-apartheid activist and fellow inmate of Nelson Mandela, has shown Robben Island to world leaders and celebrities.

2013-07-07.title: Egypt’s New Leaders Press Media to Muzzle Dissent
2013-07-07.abstract: The authorities shuttered some television stations, and the tone of some state news media also seemed to shift, to reflect the interests of those now in charge.

2013-07-07.title: Russian Official Says Venezuela Is the ‘Best Solution’ for Snowden
2013-07-07.abstract: The comments by the Russian lawmaker, Aleksei Pushkov, seemed to reflect the Kremlin’s increasing desire to be rid of Edward J. Snowden.

2013-07-07.title: Seeking to Repair a Rift in Portugal’s Ruling Coalition
2013-07-07.abstract: Paulo Portas, who resigned on Wednesday as Portugal’s foreign minister, rejoined the government on Saturday as deputy prime minister.

2013-07-07.title: After Legal Marathon, Britain Deports Terrorist Suspect
2013-07-07.abstract: The militant Islamic cleric known as Abu Qatada was deported early Sunday from Britain to his native Jordan to face terrorism charges, ending a 10-year court battle.

2013-07-07.title: Quotation of the Day for Sunday, July 7
2013-07-07.abstract: Quotation of the Day for Sunday, July 7.

2013-07-08.title: Too Hot to Exercise (and Who Really Wants To?)
2013-07-08.abstract: Qataris, who have a high obesity rate, are being urged to try to keep fit by walking in malls, where they can escape temperatures that are often over 100 degrees.

2013-07-08.title: Deadly Derailment in Quebec Underlines Oil Debate
2013-07-08.abstract: The accident, in which at least five people were killed, underscored a debate about whether it is safer to transport oil by rail or pipeline.

2013-07-08.title: Brazil Voices ‘Deep Concern’ Over Gathering of Data by U.S.
2013-07-08.abstract: Brazil’s foreign minister said his government would press the United Nations to take action that “preserves the sovereignty of all countries.”

2013-07-08.title: Islamist Party a Surprise Force in a New Egypt
2013-07-08.abstract: Al Nour, a party of ultraconservative Islamists, has emerged as an unexpected political kingmaker, shaping the interim government after the ouster of President Mohamed Morsi.

2013-07-08.title: Ex-Rail Minister in China Gets a Suspended Death Sentence
2013-07-08.abstract: Liu Zhijun, 60, a former minister of railways, confessed to taking millions in bribes in return for providing access to China’s huge spending on its train network expansion.

2013-07-09.title: Leaked Report Cites Pakistan’s Failings Before U.S. Killed Bin Laden
2013-07-09.abstract: A report that cited “collective incompetence” of the country’s security forces was leaked after Pakistani authorities had suppressed it for months.

2013-07-09.title: Chinese Journalist Is Released on Bail
2013-07-09.abstract: Du Bin said he could still face trial for accusations he said stemmed from his recent film about a Chinese labor camp and a book about the crackdown on Tiananmen Square protesters.

2013-07-09.title: In Ecuador, a Magazine’s Death Comes Amid Questions
2013-07-09.abstract: Vanguardia, Ecuador’s only weekly newsmagazine, closed after the government passed a controversial media law that opponents say will stifle investigative journalism.

2013-07-09.title: Bloody Day in Unrest Widens the Rupture Among Ordinary Egyptians
2013-07-09.abstract: The divisions have marked Cairo’s geography, with the pro-military and pro-Morsi camps occupying different squares, blocking traffic, erecting tents and rigging up loudspeakers.

2013-07-09.title: Police Eject Protesters From Istanbul Park
2013-07-09.abstract: Authorities reopened Gezi Park in Taksim Square on Monday, but police arrested scores of protesters who gathered there.

2013-07-10.title: Russia Says Study Suggests Syria Rebels Used Sarin
2013-07-10.abstract: The Russian analysis was based on evidence collected directly by Russian forensics specialists who visited a site in northern Syria, an official said.

2013-07-10.title: Salons or Not, Cyberspace Is Still a Distant Place for Most Cubans
2013-07-10.abstract: Regarded as the least wired country in the Western Hemisphere, Cuba has expanded access in Internet salons. But the prices are beyond what most Cubans can afford.

2013-07-10.title: In Portugal, a Protector of a People Is Honored
2013-07-10.abstract: A foundation is trying to bring recognition to Aristides de Sousa Mendes, a diplomat who helped thousands of people to escape Nazi persecution and later died in poverty.

2013-07-10.title: Currency Controls in Cyprus Increase Worry About Euro System
2013-07-10.abstract: Restrictions on the flow of capital have given some Cypriots the feeling that not all euros are created equal, raising a crucial question: Is the euro zone already starting to break up?

2013-07-10.title: Egypt Is Arena for Influence of Arab Rivals
2013-07-10.abstract: Two of the Persian Gulf’s richest monarchies pledged $8 billion in cash and loans to Egypt, aiming to shore up a shaky government and to compete for influence with each other and with their Islamist rivals.

2013-07-11.title: Taliban Spokesman Is Ousted for ‘Creating Mistrust’
2013-07-11.abstract: Leaflets distributed this week in North Waziristan, the main hub of Taliban militancy in Pakistan, announced that the spokesman, Sajjid Mohmand, was being replaced.

2013-07-11.title: Deaths Climb From Disaster in Quebec
2013-07-11.abstract: The police reported that the remains of 20 people had been found.

2013-07-11.title: Hopes Rise for Appeal of Ex-Marine Held in Iran
2013-07-11.abstract: Improvements in prison life for Amir Hekmati, incarcerated for nearly two years on spying accusations, strengthened his family’s hope that Iran’s judiciary would favorably review an appeal for his release.

2013-07-11.title: Israel Reopens Inquiry Into Activist’s Injury
2013-07-11.abstract: The Israeli police on Wednesday agreed to re-investigate the 2009 injury of an American activist during a clash between Israeli security forces and protesters.

2013-07-11.title: North Korea Repeats Call for Talks
2013-07-11.abstract: A senior North Korean diplomat said Wednesday that his country was willing to have “any kind of talks” to ease tensions on the Korean Peninsula.

2013-07-12.title: Siege by Taliban Strains Pakistani Girls’ Schools
2013-07-12.abstract: Since a vicious, high-profile attack on several Pakistani schoolgirls in October, the Taliban’s violent campaign against girls’ education in northwestern Pakistan has continued unabated.

2013-07-12.title: Brazil’s Workers Take to Streets in One-Day Strike
2013-07-12.abstract: The “National Day of Struggle,” organized by labor unions and focusing on issues like wages and benefits, disrupted ports and blocked some highways essential to commerce.

2013-07-12.title: U.S. and China to Discuss Investment Treaty, but Cybersecurity Is a Concern
2013-07-12.abstract: The United States and China will start negotiating a sweeping bilateral investment treaty, which could open vast sectors of their economies to investments from the other side.

2013-07-12.title: The New Royal Succession
2013-07-12.abstract: Gender will no longer be an issue for the baby of the Duke and Duchess of Cambridge under a new succession rule for the British monarchy.

2013-07-12.title: With Royal Baby Due in Britain, Street Is a Waiting Zone
2013-07-12.abstract: No due date has ever been made public for the birth of Prince William and Kate Middleton’s first child, but that hasn’t stopped crowds from forming outside St. Mary’s hospital.

2013-07-13.title: Kosovo’s Thaci Aspires to Statesmanship, but Guerrilla Past Haunts Him
2013-07-13.abstract: Prime Minister Hashim Thaci, who led Kosovo’s bloody guerrilla war in the 1990s, is being hailed for a power-sharing deal with Serbia, but his history evokes the region’s ethnic enmities.

2013-07-13.title: Islamists Step Up Protests in Egypt
2013-07-13.abstract: Hundreds of thousands of Islamist supporters rallied across Egypt pressing for Mohamed Morsi’s reinstatement.

2013-07-13.title: In Some Parts of Egypt, Bad Times Remained Bad
2013-07-13.abstract: In the impoverished Egyptian town of Fayoum, conditions never improved during Mohamed Morsi’s presidency and the future now seems unsettled and unpromising.

2013-07-13.title: Rights Groups Accuse Turkish Police of Excessive Force Against Protesters
2013-07-13.abstract: A report released by an international medical rights group indicates that Turkish police displayed “excessive and unnecessary use of force” to stop antigovernment protests.

2013-07-13.abstract: Rebel factions in Syria are attacking each other in a series of kidnappings, killings and beheadings, as competition for recruits, weapons and the right to define the character of the future state boils over.

2013-07-14.title: As Mystery Illness Stalks Its Young, India Intensifies Search for a Killer
2013-07-14.abstract: A brain swelling that is infecting the country’s youth, throwing them into comas and killing seemingly healthy children, often within hours, has stumped the country’s physicians.

2013-07-14.title: Brazilian President’s Attempts to Placate Protesters Backfire
2013-07-14.abstract: Nearly every step President Dilma Rousseff has taken has backfired, increasing public dissatisfaction with her performance.

2013-07-14.title: Japan and China Trade Sharp Words Over Islands
2013-07-14.abstract: The heated exchange over disputed territory started with a Japanese report accusing China of aggressive maritime tactics and was stark evidence of two nations uneasy about each other’s military capabilities.

2013-07-14.title: Israel Airstrike Targeted Advanced Missiles That Russia Sold to Syria, U.S. Says
2013-07-14.abstract: American officials said Israel carried out an air attack in Syria this month that targeted advanced antiship cruise missiles sold to the Syria government by Russia.

2013-07-14.title: Turkish Lawmakers Move to Curb Army’s Political Power
2013-07-14.abstract: The Turkish Parliament on Saturday amended an army regulation that had paved the way for military intervention in politics.

2013-07-15.title: Egyptian Leaders Freeze Assets of Morsi Backers
2013-07-15.abstract: Egypt’s new military-led government is increasing pressure on supporters of ousted President Mohamed Morsi to back down from their public protests.

2013-07-15.title: No Quick Impact in U.S. Arms Plan for Syria Rebels
2013-07-15.abstract: The plan calls for the C.I.A. to supply only small arms, and only to a limited segment of the opposition; the actual numbers are unclear.

2013-07-15.title: Bangladesh Pollution, Told in Colors and Smells
2013-07-15.abstract: A deadly building collapse brought the dangers of Bangladesh’s clothing manufacturing to world notice, but the environmental destruction is taking a daily toll as well.

2013-07-15.abstract: Ms. Popova was a member of a renowned unit of deadly female Soviet pilots called the “Night Witches” by German invaders because of the sound their plywood planes made.

2013-07-15.title: Environmental Damage in Bangladesh
2013-07-15.abstract: The garment and textile industries in Bangladesh have contributed heavily to what experts describe as a water pollution disaster.

2013-07-16.title: France: Captive in Mali Is Dead
2013-07-16.abstract: The death of a Frenchman held by Islamist fighters in northern Africa was confirmed on Monday after the recovery and examination of the man’s body in northern Mali.

2013-07-16.title: Finnish Tango: The Passion and the Melancholy
2013-07-16.abstract: Every July, the Finnish town of Seinajoki draws thousands of people to a four-day tango festival, and many say the dance’s blend of passion and brooding perfectly expresses the Finnish soul.

2013-07-16.title: A Finnish Town Mad for Tango
2013-07-16.abstract: Every year, the town of Seinajoki transforms several square blocks into a vast dance hall, and thousands of people gather to dance to the smoldering rhythms of the tango.

2013-07-16.title: Merkel Urges Europe to Tighten Internet Safeguards
2013-07-16.abstract: The remarks by Germany’s chancellor, Angela Merkel, reflected the anger in Europe over accounts of government surveillance by the United States National Security Agency, leaked by Edward J. Snowden.

2013-07-16.title: Drug Kingpin Is Captured in Mexico Near Border
2013-07-16.abstract: The capture of Miguel Ángel Treviño Morales, the leader of the Zetas drug cartel, was a retort from Mexico’s government to questions over whether it would go after top organized crime leaders.

2013-07-17.title: His Ship Still Aground, Captain Goes on Trial
2013-07-17.abstract: Francesco Schettino, accused of abandoning his ship after it ran aground and capsized off the Tuscan coast, could face up to 20 years in prison.

2013-07-17.title: The President of Venezuela Weds His ‘First Combatant’
2013-07-17.abstract: After resisting formally tying the knot for about two decades, President Nicolás Maduro and Cilia Flores were married on Monday, Mr. Maduro announced.

2013-07-17.title: Sudan’s President One Step Ahead of a Suit and a Warrant
2013-07-17.abstract: President Omar Hassan al-Bashir of Sudan left an African Union meeting in Nigeria after human rights groups filed a suit calling for his arrest on a warrant accusing him of genocide.

2013-07-17.abstract: With the arrest of the feared Zeta crime boss Miguel Ángel Treviño Morales, Mexico’s long and bloody drug war may have reached a crossroads.

2013-07-17.title: Off-Hour Escapes for China’s Workers
2013-07-17.abstract: The hands that make the world’s electronics belong almost entirely to young people with dreams of their own, and a lifetime of contented industrial drudgery is not among them.

2013-07-18.title: Nuclear Program Talks Could Resume, Iranian Official Says
2013-07-18.abstract: Foreign Minister Ali Akbar Salehi said Wednesday that the suspended talks could resume after Iran’s new president, Hassan Rowhani, takes office early next month.

2013-07-18.title: Greece Wins Concession on Tax for Restaurant Meals
2013-07-18.abstract: Cutting the tax to 13 percent from 23 percent, effective Aug. 1, is seen as a way to aid small businesses and bolster the tourism sector.

2013-07-18.title: Italy's Centenarian Generation
2013-07-18.abstract: As more and more Italians reach their 100th birthdays, younger generations are supporting a growing social safety net but have fewer job opportunities.

2013-07-18.title: As Population Ages, Fewer Jobs for Younger Generations
2013-07-18.abstract: Experts warn there will be fewer workers paying into the social security system to support pensioners.

2013-07-18.abstract: With older people in the Mediterranean living longer and longer lives, experts warn that Europe’s debt crisis is exacerbating a growing demographic crisis.

2013-07-19.title: Rifts Over Fees and Taliban Sour Afghanistan Exit
2013-07-19.abstract: There is a growing willingness by Afghan officials, from the president’s office down through the ministries, to publicly counter what they see as American arrogance.

2013-07-19.title: Romanian’s Tale Has Art World Fearing the Worst
2013-07-19.abstract: Ashes from the oven of a Romanian woman contain paint and nails, indicating that she may have burned seven works stolen from a Dutch museum. Her son has been arrested in the theft.

2013-07-19.title: Art World Fears Fate of Missing Masterpieces
2013-07-19.abstract: A Romanian gang behind the theft wants to cut an unspecified deal with the authorities so the artwork can be returned.

2013-07-19.title: Cuban Jews Find an Israeli Olympics With a Higher Purpose
2013-07-19.abstract: Cuba is one of 21 countries participating for the first time in the 19th Maccabiah Games, a quadrennial international sporting event known as the Jewish Olympics.

2013-07-19.abstract: In 2009, a Milan judge convicted the former C.I.A. station chief, Robert Seldon Lady, and 22 other American intelligence officials in absentia of kidnapping a Muslim cleric.

2013-07-20.title: Judge Challenges White House Claims on Authority in Drone Killings
2013-07-20.abstract: Judge Rosemary M. Collyer said she was “troubled” by the government’s assertion that it could kill American citizens it designated as dangerous with no role for judicial review.

2013-07-20.title: Europe’s Carrot-and-Stick Approach to Israel Includes Blacklisting Hezbollah
2013-07-20.abstract: The approach illustrated the European Union’s strategy of trying to rein in Israeli actions that undermine the Middle East peace process, while maintaining good relations with Israel.

2013-07-20.title: Inmate’s Letters Hint at North Korea Opening
2013-07-20.abstract: The fact that an American prisoner in North Korea was allowed to send letters to his family suggests that the reclusive state is open to negotiations about him.

2013-07-20.abstract: Liberal Democrats and their coalition partner could control both houses, ending what is known here as a “twisted Parliament” that has added to Japan’s recent political paralysis.

2013-07-20.title: A Village Mourns
2013-07-20.abstract: Poisoning from a school lunch killed 23 children from the northeastern Indian state of Bihar on Tuesday. One villager talks about the effect the tragedy had on his family.

2013-07-21.title: Former Motorcycle Driver, Paralyzed in Beating, Sets Off Bomb at Beijing Airport
2013-07-21.abstract: A man in a wheelchair, the only one hurt by the bomb, was said to have ignited it to protest government inaction after he was paralyzed in a beating by security officers.

2013-07-21.title: U.S. Intelligence Official Says Syrian War Could Last for Years
2013-07-21.abstract: A senior American intelligence official on Saturday warned that the Syrian conflict could last “many, many months to multiple years.”

2013-07-21.title: Quotation of the Day for Sunday, July 21
2013-07-21.abstract: Quotation of the Day for Sunday, July 21.

2013-07-21.title: Mexico Says Another Drug Gang Leader Is in Custody
2013-07-21.abstract: The suspect, Víctor Hugo Delgado Rentería of the Nueva Generación organization, was arrested over a week ago in the western state of Jalisco, the attorney general’s office said.

2013-07-21.title: Australia Defends New Refugee Policy, Despite Riot
2013-07-21.abstract: Following a riot in Nauru and the first arrival of a vessel under the new policy, senior officials stood firm on the choice to send all asylum seekers who arrive by boat to Papua New Guinea.

2013-07-22.title: Afghans See Their Army Woo Them With Piety
2013-07-22.abstract: Commanders are stressing their soldiers’ faith in Islam in an effort to combat the branding of the army as a secular, corrupt puppet of foreigners.

2013-07-22.title: Across Syria, Violent Day of Attacks and Ambush
2013-07-22.abstract: Government forces killed dozens of rebels in an ambush east of Damascus, fighters linked to Al Qaeda battled Kurdish militias and Syria’s military peppered an outdoor market with mortar rounds.

2013-07-22.title: Egypt Denies Morsi and U.S. Joined in a Plot
2013-07-22.abstract: The government denied an Egyptian newspaper’s report that Mohamed Morsi, the ousted president, had plotted with the American ambassador to run the country from a Cairo mosque.

2013-07-22.title: Seasoned Hand in Mideast May Shepherd Peace Talks
2013-07-22.abstract: Martin Indyk, the former American ambassador to Israel, is a leading candidate to head a new team to manage Israeli-Palestinian peace talks, diplomats said.

2013-07-22.title: Taiwan Debates Medical Parole for Ex-Leader
2013-07-22.abstract: An emotional debate is unfolding over whether Chen Shui-bian, a former president serving a 20-year prison term for corruption, should be granted medical parole.

2013-07-23.title: Japan: Nuclear Plant Admits Leaks
2013-07-23.abstract: The operator of the stricken Fukushima Daiichi nuclear plant said Monday that contaminated water had been seeping from damaged reactors into the Pacific.

2013-07-23.title: Where Police See Looted Antiquities, a Mayor Sees a Museum
2013-07-23.abstract: Investigators in Spain say a local man looted thousands of antiquities from a remote hilltop. Now the town’s mayor wants a proper excavation and then, perhaps, a museum.

2013-07-23.title: Pentagon Lays Out Options for U.S. Military Effort in Syria
2013-07-23.abstract: For the first time, the Pentagon provided explicit details to Congress about the logistics, costs and risks of options for intervening in Syria’s two-and-a-half-year-old war.

2013-07-23.title: With Modesty, Pope Francis Begins a Week in Brazil
2013-07-23.abstract: The Argentine-born pontiff, on his first international trip as pope, sidestepped the issue of Brazil’s recent protests in his first public remarks, emphasizing instead the importance of youth evangelization.

2013-07-23.title: Brazil's Seeds of Protest
2013-07-23.abstract: Brazilians express their grievances with the lack of resources invested in education and health care.

2013-07-24.title: Harsh Self-Assessment as Cuba Looks Within
2013-07-24.abstract: President Raúl Castro and many of his citizens are bemoaning a loss of culture and civility in the nation.

2013-07-24.title: Mexico: Money Laundering Suspects Blacklisted
2013-07-24.abstract: Ratcheting up pressure on the Zetas, the United States has added two of its top operatives to a blacklist that freezes their assets and bars Americans from doing business with them.

2013-07-24.title: Flooded German Area Rebuilds, as Government Aid Lags
2013-07-24.abstract: Angela Merkel has pledged to reduce the bureaucracy affecting citizens, but some in an area hit by flooding say getting government help has been slowed by onerous requirements.

2013-07-24.title: Japan’s Leader Urges Swift Action to Strengthen Economic Recovery
2013-07-24.abstract: The appeals for urgency appear to reflect the extent to which Prime Minister Shinzo Abe and his party now see themselves as under pressure to deliver on campaign promises.

2013-07-24.title: As Croatia Struggles, Some Wonder if It Won Entry to European Union Too Soon
2013-07-24.abstract: Croatian officials long lobbied to get into the European Union, but some Croatians worry that the country is not ready.

2013-07-25.title: Scores Reported Dead as Train Derails in Spain
2013-07-25.abstract: A high-speed passenger train crashed in northwestern Spain on Wednesday night, killing at least 77 people and leaving at least 100 injured, according to judicial sources and local news reports.

2013-07-25.title: In Quebec Town, Fires Are Out, but Recovery Is Distant
2013-07-25.abstract: More than two weeks after a runaway train of oil tank cars derailed and exploded in Lac-Mégantic, the decontamination effort has erased relatively little of the destruction.

2013-07-25.title: Bulgarian Police Break Up Protesters’ Blockade of Parliament
2013-07-25.abstract: After weeks of demonstrations against the government and after protesters trapped about 100 lawmakers in the building, the police moved in to free the officials.

2013-07-25.title: Missteps by Brazil Mar Visit by Pope
2013-07-25.abstract: Pope Francis celebrated his first public Mass on Wednesday, but his visit to Brazil continued to be plagued by tension over gaffes by its Brazilian organizers.

2013-07-25.title: Syria: Chemical Arms Experts Arrive
2013-07-25.abstract: Two United Nations officials arrived in Syria on Wednesday to discuss the scope of an inquiry into the suspected use of chemical weapons in the war.

2013-07-26.title: In Cambodia, Shifting Allegiances Shape a Tougher Than Usual Election
2013-07-26.abstract: Despite Prime Minister Hun Sen’s ability to overcome the resistance of even his toughest enemies, he is finding new challenges from other quarters of Cambodian society.

2013-07-26.title: Taliban Seize Police Force in a Hamlet
2013-07-26.abstract: An entire local police unit disappeared in a small Afghan village, and the Taliban claimed responsibility.

2013-07-26.title: Chinese Search for Infant Formula Goes Global
2013-07-26.abstract: Fueled by distrust of domestic brands, Chinese parents are buying up foreign-made infant milk powder, and that has led to shortages in at least a half-dozen other countries.

2013-07-26.title: European Court Says Russia Did Not Persecute Ex-Tycoon
2013-07-26.abstract: The judges said the charges against Mikhail B. Khodorkovsky were sufficient to explain his prosecution, but added that his rights were violated in other ways.

2013-07-26.title: A Chinese Demand for Foreign Formula
2013-07-26.abstract: A tainted baby formula scandal in 2008 has Chinese parents obsessed with buying foreign-made milk powder.

2013-07-27.title: Soldiers’ Mass Execution Reported by Syria Group
2013-07-27.abstract: Extremists in Syria’s insurgency killed 150 government fighters, including 51 who were executed after they had surrendered, a monitoring group said.

2013-07-27.title: A Trail of Masterpieces and a Web of Lies, Leading to Anguish
2013-07-27.abstract: Investigators are still struggling to penetrate the fog of claims and counterclaims about what happened to seven paintings that were stolen by a Romanian criminal gang from a Dutch museum.

2013-07-27.title: On the Trail of Stolen Masterpieces
2013-07-27.abstract: Investigators have been unable to locate seven works stolen from a Dutch museum by a Romanian thief and his gang.

2013-07-27.title: President-Elect Stirs Optimism in Iran and West
2013-07-27.abstract: Hassan Rouhani is concerned about his country’s growing economic troubles and is determined to change policies that have cut off relations with most of the developed world.

2013-07-27.title: Demonstrations Grow in Egypt
2013-07-27.abstract: Friends and foes of Mohamed Morsi, the ousted president, turned out for rallies across the country.

2013-07-28.title: Taliban Gain Foothold in a Pakistani City
2013-07-28.abstract: Hounded by the army in the tribal belt, the Pakistani Taliban are spilling into Peshawar, stepping up attacks on the police, extortion demands, sectarian killings and kidnappings.

2013-07-28.title: Chinese, With Revamped Force, Make Presence Known in East China Sea
2013-07-28.abstract: China sent four ships to patrol waters off disputed islands in the nearby East China Sea, and sent a turboprop early-warning aircraft through international airspace between the islands of Okinawa and Miyako.

2013-07-28.title: Netanyahu Agrees to Free 104 Palestinians
2013-07-28.abstract: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel announced on Saturday that he had agreed to release the prisoners to pave the way for a resumption of Israeli-Palestinian negotiations in Washington.

2013-07-28.title: South Korea Pledges Millions in Aid for North
2013-07-28.abstract: The announcement coincided with a call by Seoul for “one last round” of make-or-break talks on restarting a jointly operated industrial complex.

2013-07-28.title: Israel and Palestinians Set to Resume Peace Talks, U.S. Announces
2013-07-28.abstract: The meeting between negotiators, planned to start Monday and announced by the State Department, will be the first time that the two have held direct talks since 2010.

2013-07-29.title: Mali Holds Elections After Year of Turmoil
2013-07-29.abstract: Under pressure from the West to restore a semblance of democracy, voting proceeds peacefully. An August runoff is likely.

2013-07-29.title: A Link Straight to Syria’s Ancient Past Endures as War Creeps Closer
2013-07-29.abstract: Straight Street has been known since at least the early years of Christianity for its ramrod course through the twisting alleys of the old city of Damascus, but now residents fear for its very existence.

2013-07-29.title: This Heroine Wears a Burqa to Fight Evil
2013-07-29.abstract: A new cartoon in Pakistan features a role model for female empowerment, but her costume has stirred some controversy.

2013-07-29.title: White House Muted in Response to New Mass Killing of Egyptian Protesters
2013-07-29.abstract: Democratic and Republican lawmakers, while condemning the second mass killing of demonstrators following the ouster of President Mohamed Morsi, stopped short of calling for cutting aid to Egypt.

2013-07-29.title: Syrian Opposition Leader Says He Would Meet Assad Officials
2013-07-29.abstract: Ahmad al-Jarba, president of the National Coalition of Syrian Revolutionary and Opposition Forces, says he is prepared to attend a peace conference in Geneva without preconditions.

2013-07-30.title: Mediating as French Culture and Economics Collide
2013-07-30.abstract: Aurélie Filippetti, a novelist, is finding new kinds of criticism as France’s minister of culture and communication.

2013-07-30.title: Militants Attack Prison in Northwest Pakistan
2013-07-30.abstract: The concerted assault resulted in prisoners escaping, said a senior security official in the town of Dera Ismail Khan, where the prison is, but exact numbers are not known.

2013-07-30.title: Tunisia Faces More Anger After an Ambush Kills Soldiers
2013-07-30.abstract: At least eight soldiers were killed on the country’s northwestern border, an attack that comes amid large protests against the Islamist-led government.

2013-07-30.title: Navy Officer’s Killing Escalates Fight for Mexican State
2013-07-30.abstract: Two months after Mexico’s president sent troops to Michoacán, gunmen killed one of the Mexican Navy’s most senior officers, raising the stakes in a battle for control of the area.

2013-07-30.title: 13th-Century Alleyways and a Modern Plague of Illegal Renovations
2013-07-30.abstract: Beijing is losing many of its hutongs, the traditional Chinese alleyways that date to the 13th century, and gaining a bevy of illegal, outsize construction in their places.

2013-07-31.title: Mugabe Keeps Grip Before Vote, but the Opposition Grows Bolder
2013-07-31.abstract: President Robert Mugabe of Zimbabwe does not appear ready to give up power, but says that he would go if he loses Wednesday’s election.

2013-07-31.title: Political Inquiry Makes Judge a Star, as It Reveals Flaws in Spain’s Courts
2013-07-31.abstract: Spanish courts are overwhelmed, easily politicized and prone to debilitating delays, which is eroding the public’s trust.

2013-07-31.title: Success Brings Scrutiny to Chinese Mystic
2013-07-31.abstract: Wang Lin, a practitioner of qigong, says Chinese government charges of swindling, dodging taxes and cavorting with criminals are part of a vendetta against him.

2013-07-31.title: Fatal Crash Came While Train Driver Took a Call
2013-07-31.abstract: Data from two “black boxes” show that the driver, Francisco Jose Garzon Amo, was on the phone with a colleague and traveling at almost twice the speed limit.

2013-07-31.title: A French Dining Staple Is Losing Its Place at the Table
2013-07-31.abstract: The decline in bread consumption is so worrisome that a nationwide campaign is championing bread as promoting good health, good conversation and French civilization.
